title: Greetings & Goodbyes
shortSummary: Greet people, introduce yourself, and say goodbye in Korean, the foundation of every conversation.
culturalNote: Korean greetings are tied to politeness levels and a slight bow. The everyday polite greeting is 안녕하세요 (annyeonghaseyo), used for hello at any time of day with people you do not know well, and it comes with a small bow of the head. With close friends of the same age you can drop to the casual 안녕 (annyeong). Age and seniority matter enormously: you raise your level of politeness for anyone older or in a position above you, and a deeper bow shows greater respect. When saying goodbye, Korean splits into two phrases depending on who is leaving: say 안녕히 가세요 (annyeonghi gaseyo, go in peace) to the person leaving, and 안녕히 계세요 (annyeonghi gyeseyo, stay in peace) if you are the one leaving. Getting this pair right is a small thing that native speakers notice and appreciate.

keyPhrases.context: Polite default, any time of day

grammar.explanation: Most everyday Korean speech ends in -요 (yo), which makes a sentence polite without being stiffly formal. Learners should default to this level. Dropping the -요 makes speech casual and is only safe with close friends or those clearly younger.
grammar.commonMistake: Dropping -요 with someone older or a stranger, which sounds rude or overly familiar.

grammar.explanation: Korean chooses the goodbye phrase based on who is moving. Say 안녕히 가세요 (annyeonghi gaseyo) to someone who is leaving while you stay, and 안녕히 계세요 (annyeonghi gyeseyo) when you yourself are leaving and they stay.
grammar.commonMistake: Using 가세요 when you are the one walking out the door; you should say 계세요.

dialogue.setup: Minji meets her new colleague Mr Park at the office in the morning.

lines.speaker: 민지
lines.english: Nice to meet you. I'm Minji.

lines.speaker: 박 과장님
lines.english: Pleased to meet you. I'm Park.

quiz.question: How do you say 'Hello' (polite) in Korean?
quiz.explanation: 안녕하세요 (annyeonghaseyo) is the everyday polite greeting used at any time of day.

quiz.question: Which phrase do you say to a person who is leaving while you stay?
quiz.explanation: 안녕히 가세요 (annyeonghi gaseyo) means 'go in peace' and is said to the one departing.

quiz.question: 잘 _____? (polite 'how are you')
quiz.explanation: 잘 지내세요? (jal jinaeseyo) asks 'how are you?' politely.

quiz.question: Which greeting is the most formal?
quiz.explanation: 안녕하십니까 (annyeonghasimnikka) is the formal hello used in business and announcements.

quiz.question: Arrange: 반갑습니다 / 만나서 (pleased to meet you)
quiz.explanation: 만나서 반갑습니다 (mannaseo bangapseumnida) means 'pleased to meet you'.
